Metadata.title: Quiet Riots
Metadata.description: Samaippa vikkapugal neruppu. Unakku saadhana manushan kanom. Samaliley irupi vali seyrom.

Footer.mission: Unakku vaalkai kaalaththil sollatha vaalkai vazhkai vittu nalla vaalkai vazhka mudiyum enbadhai seyrom.
Footer.powered: Quiet Riots · Powered by OpenClaw AI Agent

Home.headline: Vali sollatha maraipu vali vellum
Home.description: Unakku sapai vikkapugal irukkum manushan kanom. Samaliley irupi vali seyrom. Quiet Riots manushan sambantham vikkapugal solliya vali vellum prayogangaliley saadhana karangal seyrom.
Home.findYourIssueDesc: Enai vikaippugal thandum - trains, broadband, NHS, climate. Irundhaal engal vittum epdi niraiya manushan idu vikkapukal solliya arivillai.
Home.useThePivotDesc: Samaippa vikkapukal ellai organisationangalai kaanum, illai ellai vikkapukal onru organisationai kaanum. Tango aadiyal maariyal poley.
Home.takeActionDesc: Ariviyal, vali, samaliley irupi - unakku veruppa samayam ariviyal usavum.
Home.missionQuote: "Unakku vaalkai kaalaththil sollatha vaalkai vazhkai vittu nalla vaalkai vazhka mudiyum enbadhai seyrom."
Home.riotersFunny: {count} vali solliya manushan idu funny sollinangal

RejectionReasons.title: Why Quiet Riots Get Rejected
RejectionReasons.subtitle: Understanding the review process
RejectionReasons.intro: Every suggestion for a new Quiet Riot is reviewed by a Setup Guide to ensure it fits the platform and can make a real difference. Here are the reasons a suggestion might not be approved:
RejectionReasons.closeToExistingTitle: Close to an Existing Quiet Riot
RejectionReasons.closeToExistingP1: If your suggestion is very similar to a Quiet Riot that already exists, we'll merge your suggestion into the existing one. This keeps the community focused and prevents fragmentation. The more people behind one Quiet Riot, the bigger the impact.
RejectionReasons.closeToExistingP2: When this happens, you'll be automatically added to the existing Quiet Riot so you can start taking action right away.
RejectionReasons.aboutPeopleP1: Quiet Riots are about shared issues, not about targeting individuals. We focus on systemic problems — things like poor broadband, rising energy costs, or inadequate healthcare — rather than personal grievances with specific people.
RejectionReasons.aboutPeopleP2: If you're frustrated with a specific person or public figure, try framing it as the underlying issue instead. For example, instead of targeting a CEO, suggest a Quiet Riot about the company's practices.
RejectionReasons.illegalP1: We can't host Quiet Riots that promote or organise illegal activities. This includes anything that violates applicable laws, promotes violence, or could put people at risk.
RejectionReasons.otherP1: Occasionally a suggestion might not fit for another reason — perhaps it's too vague, or the Setup Guide needs more information. In these cases, you'll receive a message explaining why and what you can do next.
RejectionReasons.whatNextIntro: If your suggestion is rejected, you can always:
RejectionReasons.reframe: Reframe and try again — adjust the name or description to better fit the guidelines
RejectionReasons.joinExisting: Join an existing Quiet Riot — your issue might already be covered
RejectionReasons.replyGuide: Reply to the Setup Guide — if you think the rejection was a mistake, send a message explaining your case

AnonymousInfo.title: Anonymous First Rioters
AnonymousInfo.subtitle: What being anonymous means
AnonymousInfo.intro: When someone starts a new Quiet Riot, they become the First Quiet Rioter. This is a badge of honour — the person who had the courage to speak up first.
AnonymousInfo.choosingIntro: Not everyone wants their name attached to an issue publicly. That's completely fine. When the First Quiet Rioter chooses to stay anonymous:
AnonymousInfo.choosingHidden: Their name and profile picture are hidden from the issue page
AnonymousInfo.choosingBadge: An anonymous badge is shown instead, so people know someone started it without knowing who
AnonymousInfo.choosingGuides: The Setup Guides still know who started it (for moderation purposes)
AnonymousInfo.choosingChange: The First Rioter can change their mind later and choose to be publicly recognised
AnonymousInfo.whyIntro: There are many valid reasons to stay anonymous:
AnonymousInfo.whySensitive: The issue is sensitive or controversial
AnonymousInfo.whyWork: You work for a company related to the issue
AnonymousInfo.whyCause: You prefer to let the cause speak for itself
AnonymousInfo.changingP1: If you started a Quiet Riot anonymously but later decide you want to be recognised, you can update your preference at any time through your inbox or by messaging a Setup Guide.

FirstRioterInfo.responsibilityEvidence: Evidence stories share panni problem explain pannum

FirstRioterInfo.recognitionAnonymous: Anonymous — anonymous badge show pannuvom, identity safe

FirstRioterInfo.howP1: Pullai Quiet Riot suggest panni vaithudum, website or WhatsApp bot. Setup Guides approve panandhuvom First Quiet Rioter aam.
FirstRioterInfo.howP2: Suggestion review process vokkam, Setup Guides guidelines check pannum. Info ketta merge pannam, approved go live pannam.

Share.title: Your Quiet Riots Share
Share.profileLink: Learn more about your Quiet Riots share →
Share.heroSubtitle: Every Quiet Rioter worldwide is offered one share in Quiet Riots — the company itself.
Share.ownershipTitle: What Does Owning a Share Mean?
Share.ownershipDesc: As a shareholder, you own a real piece of Quiet Riots. This is not a token, a badge, or a metaphor — it is a genuine share in a UK limited company, registered with Companies House.
Share.ownershipPoint1: You become a legal co-owner of Quiet Riots
Share.ownershipPoint2: Your share grows in value as the company grows
Share.ownershipPoint3: You may receive dividends if the company distributes profits
Share.ownershipPoint4: You have shareholder rights under UK company law
Share.considerationDesc: Under UK company law (Companies Act 2006), shares cannot be issued for free. A nominal payment — called the consideration — is required for the share to be legally valid.
Share.considerationAmount: The consideration for your share is 10 pence (or the equivalent in your local currency).
Share.considerationWallet: This is deducted from your Quiet Riots wallet when you proceed.
Share.considerationRefund: If your application is rejected, the 10p is automatically refunded to your wallet.
Share.growthSharesTitle: How This Works at Different Valuations
Share.growthSharesDesc: Your share uses a growth share structure — a special class that only becomes valuable above a certain company valuation. This means minimal tax at the time of issue.
Share.preSeedDesc: Shares issued at nominal value. You pay 10p. No meaningful tax event.
Share.seedDesc: Same nominal value. Growth share hurdle set above current valuation.
Share.seriesADesc: Pre-emption rights waived for user share pool. Hurdle minimises current value.
Share.seriesBDesc: Growth share hurdle ensures limited current value despite high company valuation.
Share.seriesCDesc: User share pool is a defined cap table percentage. No new dilutive issuances.
Share.ipoDesc: User shares convert to ordinary tradeable shares. Capital gains tax may apply on sale.
Share.responsibilitiesDesc: Owning a share comes with some responsibilities:
Share.responsibility1: Tax obligations depend on your country of residence — consult a qualified advisor
Share.responsibility2: There is no guarantee the company will reach any valuation
Share.responsibility3: The share structure may be modified as the company grows
Share.responsibility4: This is not legal, tax, or investment advice
Share.disclaimer1: This is a simplified explanation — not legal, tax, or investment advice.
Share.disclaimer2: Each user's tax obligations depend on their country of residence.
Share.disclaimer3: There is no guarantee the company will reach any valuation.
Share.disclaimer4: The share structure may be modified as the company grows.
Share.disclaimer5: Users should consult qualified advisors in their own country.
Share.eligibilityDesc: To qualify for the share offer, you need to:
Share.eligibilityVerified: Be a verified user (name and email or phone confirmed)
Share.eligibilityMet: You are eligible for your share!
Share.proceedDesc: Ready to become a shareholder? The 10p consideration will be deducted from your wallet.
Share.proceedConfirm: A nominal payment of 10p will be deducted from your wallet. This is a legal requirement for the share to be valid.
Share.proceedSuccess: Your application has been submitted! A Share Guide will review it shortly.
Share.insufficientFunds: You need at least 10p in your wallet to proceed.
Share.declineDesc: If you don't want a share, you can permanently decline the offer. No payment will be taken.
Share.declineConfirm: Are you sure? This is permanent and cannot be undone. You will never be offered a share again.
Share.declineSuccess: You have declined the share offer.
Share.withdrawDesc: Cancel your application and receive a full refund of your 10p.
Share.withdrawConfirm: Are you sure? Your 10p will be refunded to your wallet.
Share.withdrawSuccess: Your application has been withdrawn. 10p has been refunded to your wallet.
Share.reapplyDesc: Your previous application was rejected. You can reapply with a fresh 10p payment.
Share.passwordDesc: The share section is password-protected for your security.
Share.identityDesc: To complete your share application, we need to verify your identity. This information is encrypted and stored securely.
Share.identitySuccess: Your identity has been submitted for verification.
Share.identityMustBe18: You must be at least 18 years old.
Share.issueEligibilityBanner: You are eligible for your share
Share.issueRequestReceived: Your request has been received. We will be in contact in due course.
